sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the left green square moved to the right side of the left box (it was centered, now it's on the right edge). The right green square also moved right. So RIGHT moves both green squares even when one is inside a box. This means I need to be careful about moving the right green square without displacing the left one from its box.
Let me check the exact positions to understand the movement pattern better.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions after RIGHT
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Board region (rows 25-38, cols 8-55):")
for r in range(25, 39):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r:3d}: {ascii_lines[r][8:56]}")
# Check segmentation
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nBoxes and green squares:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'c'] and n['pixels'] < 30:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, bounds: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Left green square (id=11): rows 33-35, cols 15-17 - moved from cols 12-14 to cols 15-17 (right by 3)
- Left box (id=9): rows 32-36, cols 11-15, children=[] - NO children now! The green square moved out of the box!
- Right green square (id=6): rows 30-32, cols 27-29 - didn't move! Wait, that's the same as before.
Wait, let me reconsider. The left green square was at cols 12-14 and moved to cols 15-17. But the box is at cols 11-15. So the green square is now partially outside the box (cols 15-17 vs box cols 11-15). The box's children list is empty, confirming the green square is no longer inside.
But the right green square stayed at cols 27-29. That's strange - earlier RIGHT moved both green squares. Why didn't it move this time?
Wait, let me check the previous state. Before this RIGHT:
- Left green: rows 33-35, cols 12-14
- Right green: rows 30-32, cols 27-29
After RIGHT:
- Left green: rows 33-35, cols 15-17
- Right green: rows 30-32, cols 27-29
So only the left green square moved! The right green square didn't move. This is different from before when both moved.
Hmm, maybe the rule is: RIGHT moves the "active" green square. When both are outside boxes, both move. When one is in a box, only the one outside moves? But the left green square was in the box and it moved...
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the rule is based on which green square is "selected" or "active". Or maybe the rule is:
- When both green squares are at the same row, both move horizontally
- When they're at different rows, only one moves
Let me check: before this RIGHT, the left green was at rows 33-35 and the right green was at rows 30-32. They're at different rows! So maybe horizontal movement only affects the green square that's at the "active" row.
Actually, let me reconsider the movement rules from the beginning:
1. Initially, both green squares were at rows 30-32 (same row). RIGHT moved both.
2. After DOWN, the left green moved to rows 33-35 (different row from right green at 30-32).
3. After RIGHT, only the left green moved (from cols 12-14 to cols 15-17).
So the rule might be: horizontal movement only affects green squares at the "control" row, which changes based on vertical movement.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. Maybe the rule is simpler:
- UP/DOWN moves the left green square (the "active" one)
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the green square that's NOT in a box
When the left green was in the box (cols 12-14, inside box cols 11-15), pressing RIGHT moved it out of the box. But the right green didn't move because... hmm.
Let me think differently. Maybe:
- The "active" green square is the one that's NOT in a box
- LEFT/RIGHT moves the active green square
- UP/DOWN moves the active green square
When both are outside boxes, both move. When one is in a box, only the one outside moves.
But in this case, the left green was in the box and it moved. So that doesn't work either.
Let me just try to understand by experiment. Let me try RIGHT again to see what happens.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The left green square moved out of the box when I pressed RIGHT
# The right green square didn't move (stayed at cols 27-29)
# This is different from before when both moved
# Let me try RIGHT again to see the pattern
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
